content.problemStatement:
You are given a frequency array FFF of size NNN. An array AAA of size (F1+F2+‚Ä¶+FN)(F_1 + F_2 + \ldots + F_N)(F1‚Äã+F2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+FN‚Äã) is called good if it contains the integer XXX exactly FXF_XFX‚Äã times, for each 1‚â§X‚â§N1 \le X \le N1‚â§X‚â§N.

The score of an array AAA is defined as min‚Å°(LIS(A),LDS(A))\min(LIS(A), LDS(A))min(LIS(A),LDS(A)), where LIS(A)LIS(A)LIS(A) denotes the longest (strictly) increasing subsequence of AAA, and LDS(A)LDS(A)LDS(A) denotes the longest (strictly) decreasing subsequence of AAA.

Over all good arrays, find the maximum possible score.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the size of the frequency array FFF.
The second line contains NNN integers - F1,F2,‚Ä¶,FNF_1, F_2, \ldots, F_NF1‚Äã,F2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,FN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum score of a good array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We can choose the array A=[1,2,3,1]A = [1, 2, 3, 1]A=[1,2,3,1], and this has LIS=3LIS = 3LIS=3 ([1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3]) and LDS=2LDS = 2LDS=2 ([3,1][3, 1][3,1]). Thus, it has a score of 222.
Test Case 2 : We can choose the array A=[1,2,1,2]A = [1, 2, 1, 2]A=[1,2,1,2], and this has LIS=LDS=2LIS = LDS = 2LIS=LDS=2.
